==Description==
[[File:TheBeginning.jpg|link=]] Isolation surrounds you.
'''The Beginning''' is a rather peculiar anomalous level, said to be connected to [[The End]] and [[The Middle]] in some way. The level is hypothesized to have existed for a very long time in the Backrooms, hence its name. It is speculated that '''the Beginning''' is responsible for the creation of some levels and [[The Corrupted Instability|failed creations]], although this theory is not proven. In physical appearance, '''the Beginning''' is a network of dimly lit rooms, resembling a vintage office complex with a grey carpet and pale colored walls plus tile-patterned ceilings. The majority of these rooms lack any sort of furniture nor objects, giving off a desolated-like aura. Periodically, one may stumble upon empty notebooks on the floor or cubicles devoid of anything within them. A select number of rooms contain electrical sockets and vents, albeit the electrical sockets output miniscule volts of electricity, and the vents are too small for a human to fit in. As one ventures further into '''the Beginning's''' network of rooms, relatively large cracks and holes revealing an [[The Void|endless abyss]] will begin to appear; falling into these holes will directly lead one to [[the Void]]. The holes and cracks imply '''the Beginning''' being possibly incomplete. No-clipping does not function as it typically does, as well. One may come upon a room containing multiple objects: a filing cabinet, a chair, an empty bottle, and a 1980s-esque computer. The filing cabinet contains labeled folders with documents on every anomalous location in the Backrooms. The documents are synced with the pages in the M.E.G. Database; both sharing the same information and images. These documents somehow are updated in time with the database. This phenomenon is still being investigated. The documents are reportedly challenging to tamper with, being considered as near impossible. Nonetheless, attempting to damage or keep these documents results in the wanderer being glitched out of '''the Beginning''' into a random level, and the documents being returned back unharmed. The computer is quite unique; whilst not possessing the exact capabilities as [[Entity PC]] in [[the End]], it may still configure aspects of '''the Beginning'''. Furthermore, the computer can reveal certain stats of the Backrooms. These actions can be performed by typing particular commands that execute their respective function. Strangely, aside from a disk tray, the computer doesn't feature any external ports. Should the computer be booted up, it will display a black and lime terminal. The appearance of the terminal is immutable. As mentioned earlier, the computer can only modify objects, entities, or aspects of its level, and not any entities, objects or levels outside of '''the Beginning'''. As an example, attempting to delete an entity outside of '''the Beginning''' will result in failure; on the other hand, deleting an entity that is present within the confines of the level will work. Although, some commands have extremely rare exceptions. The commands will only apply to the wanderer who executes them, and nobody else should anyone else be in the level. Moreover, the computer will ignore any command or keys the user inputs that isn't available, effectively meaning the computer is immune to viruses and malware.
[[File:40ServerRoom.webp|link=]] Static Feelings.
One may find doors scattered throughout '''the Beginning''', which all lead to relatively medium sized, organized server rooms. In contrast to the rest of '''the Beginning''', these server rooms are completely white in color, with a reflective floor and an overall more modernized appearance. Nevertheless, cracks can still be seen in the ceilings, and climbing through these will bring you to [[the Void]]. The server boxes glow a neon blue light, though the boxes have an inconsistent brightness, with some being brighter than other. Additionally, the hum-buzz of the fluorescent lights is more prevalent here. Old hardware such as laptops, computers and telephones can be found stored within the crates; however, these electronics don't seem to work. The server boxes are powered by the electrical sockets, which may be placed in unordinary spots in the server rooms' walls. The cord connecting the server boxes to the outlets cannot by any means be disrupted. Unlike the electrical outlets outside the server rooms, the ones here supply a way higher amount of voltage. The data stored in the server boxes is yet to be determined, as not even the computer in the level mentions nor can access the data in the server rooms. Many have suggested the data stored in the server boxes is from the commands of the computer, saving data of whoever used the computer and a log of commands they have used in the past. Others have also suggested that because of the level's given title, the data stored in the server boxes may be a collection of information of the Backrooms. === Commands === The following is a list of all documented commands one may input in '''the Beginning's''' computer: ====Default Commands==== */spawn - Typing this followed by an underscore and a number or name in caps will spawn in a hologram visualizing the corresponding entity; the holograms are unable to be interacted with. */transport - Typing this followed by a number/title will briefly knock out the person, and immediately send them to that particular level. */delete - Typing this followed by "LEVEL_" or "ENTITY_" and an integer/title will result in receiving an error message. */clear - Clears the console. */exit - Typing this or any other related command will be met with an error message. ====Additional Commands====
[[File:LolWhatIsThis.png|link=]] The Terminal.
*/help - Lists almost all available commands, along with a brief description for each one. */undo - Reverts the last command, and depending on the command, will return the screen back to the terminal. Executing this command after it has already been executed redoes the command it previously undid. */visualize - Typing this followed by either LEVEL_(integer/title) or a location in [[the Frontrooms]] will display the respective area. */patch - Covers up the nearest broken parts of the level infrastructure. */illuminate - Brightens up the room the computer is in. The lights will dim back to normal once the wanderer inputs another command. */dim - Does the opposite of /illuminate. The lights will brighten back to normal once the wanderer inputs another command. */almond - Summons a singular full bottle of [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. This command can be used as much as wanted; however, the Almond water itself cannot be brought out of the level, although the effects applied to the wanderer if consumed will remain, regardless of if one exits '''the Beginning''' or not. */karma - Reveals the [[Level 995|karma rating]] of the wanderer. However, if the command is used again each time, the rating will slowly decrease by 2Some have speculated this to be a measure to prevent wanderers from "farming" karma, but the real reason for this is unknown.. */manifest - Typing this followed by an integer or a title will cause the entire level to manifest into a wavy and blurry version of that particular level. The hazards from the actual level being manifested will not be present. In addition, the computer will disappear, meaning no-clipping is the only way to exit out of the manifestation, leading you to [[the Corrupted Instability]]. */invert - Makes the wanderer's perspective of the level inverted in color. */preventBSOD - (case sensitive command) Should one do this command, the next time they enter [[Level 111]] the Rebooting... event will not occur. This command can only be used once per month. */time - Displays the current time of a random time zone on the Earth in [[the Frontrooms]]. */footage - Shows a video recording of [[Level 0]]; it is unknown how the footage was recorded, since the level is devoid of any cameras. */music - plays random music. The music that is playing cannot be changed by the user. ===Additional Information=== A popular theory regarding '''the Beginning''' states the level was thought to serve a role in the creation and modification of some levels, entities, and objects, plus, stored information regarding the Backrooms. This is evident through the available commands of the computer (e.g: "/delete" and "/spawn") and the server rooms. In some point of time, this role may've by unknown means been switched over to [[Entity PC]], almost nullifying '''the Beginning's''' computer's ability to affect anything outside its confines. This could be a valid reason as to why certain commands appear "downgraded" or give an error message when attempting to be used. Although no one understands exactly why The Beginning has lost this ability, it is generally thought that any harm to any of the levels cannot be completely established by either the [[M.E.G.]] or any other wanderer for that matter. This is since it's possible that The Beginning could've possibly been compromised in a manner unknown by some sort of corruption. == Addendum ==
{{Quote|It appears that the computer was more than I had believed. I thought the computer was going to be dangerous, but it ended up not being a problem. I'll concede that it was a bit more complex than anticipated, but not as damaging as [[Entity PC]].

Despite all this, no change has been seen in the levels. When I went back to Level 0, I could sense that something wasn't right. I felt like glitches were coming from behind me, but it turns out I was probably stuck on [[The Corrupted Instability|that one level]]. Maybe this was all in my head? Who else could come to know it?

Given the number of unstable levels I've come across, it's possible the corruption must've played tricks on me; so I'll be writing this note to inform anyone who sees it. "If you're ever relocated to a safer place, please be aware that it may take some time to get used to the new surroundings. There is no danger whatsoever associated with this activity.|R. "Manuel" Aindi.}}
==Entities== '''The Beginning''' is normally devoid of entities. However, one may summon holograms of the majority of the entities of the Backrooms via commands. ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to '''the Beginning's''' non-linear and non-Euclidean geometry, wanderers aren't able to meet each other, nor inflict any effects on each other via the level's computer. Thus, colonies and outposts are infeasible to establish. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *The main and only documented way to access this level is through [[Level 111]], suggesting a connection between both levels. === Exits === *Typing /transport_(number/title) will have a chance of bringing you to the respective level. **The higher the survival difficulty of the level is, the higher the chance. **Typing /transport_Frontrooms will trap you in [[Level 398]]. *No-clipping into the computer will take you to [[Level 0]]. *Falling through the holes found in the level will bring you directly to [[the Void]]. *Pressing Alt + F4 on the keyboard will take you to [[Level 111]]. *Typing /end will make the computer disappear, and the level will morph into [[The End|the final space]]. *Attempting to escape the manifestation will bring you to [[the Corrupted Instability]]. {{Footnotes}}
